title: Cosmos Runner — jump and duck a glowing runner past endless obstacles
summary: A real, self-contained endless-runner (dino-style) arcade game in a single HTML file with zero dependencies. A glowing runner auto-runs to the right while you jump over ground obstacles and duck under high bars; your distance is the score and the run keeps speeding up. Hitting any obstacle crashes the runner and ends the run, then you restart. Playable with keyboard and touch. It also ships a deterministic self-playing attract demo for capture: a seeded AI jumps and ducks the seeded obstacle field perfectly, rendered as a pure function of time via window.__seek(t).
usage_notes: RUN: double-click index.html. CONTROLS: SPACE / ArrowUp / tap (upper area) to jump; ArrowDown / tap-and-hold (lower third) to duck; R key or the Restart button to start over after a crash. GOAL: run as far as possible — score = distance; the speed steadily increases, and touching any obstacle ends the run. KEY TECHNIQUES: the game is a fixed-timestep (16ms) deterministic simulation; obstacle spacing and type come from a mulberry32-seeded stream keyed by obstacle index, and distance is the closed-form integral of an accelerating speed, so AUTO replays identically; the seeded auto-player looks ahead to the nearest obstacle and jumps cacti/low blocks or ducks under high bars at the correct lead distance; AUTO re-simulates from a fixed initial state to t each seek (N small) so the frame is a pure function of time, with a START offset so frame 0 is already mid-run jumping an obstacle with score>0. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 self-plays + window.__seek(t). TUNE: GRAV, JUMP, BASESPEED, SPEEDGAIN, obstacle gap/type ranges, the auto jump/duck lead windows, and LOOP.
